import re, unicodedata, json, math, statistics, itertools

# Arabic diacritics range including shadda, sukun, etc.
DIACRITICS = ''.join(chr(c) for c in range(0x0610, 0x061A+1)) + ''.join(chr(c) for c in range(0x064B, 0x065F+1)) + '\u0670' + '\u06D6' + '\u06DC' + '\u06DF' + '\u06E0' + '\u06E1' + '\u06E2' + '\u06E3' + '\u06E4' + '\u06E7' + '\u06E8' + '\u06EA' + '\u06EB' + '\u06EC' + '\u06ED'
DIACRITICS_RE = re.compile('[' + re.escape(DIACRITICS) + ']')

def normalize_ar(s):
    if s is None:
        return ''
    # normalize unicode, remove diacritics, tatweel, punctuation
    s = unicodedata.normalize('NFC', s)
    s = s.replace('ٱ','ا').replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا')
    s = s.replace('ى','ي').replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي').replace('ة','ه')
    s = s.replace('ـ','')  # tatweel
    s = DIACRITICS_RE.sub('', s)
    # punctuation: remove Arabic and Latin punctuation
    s = re.sub(r'[^\w\s\u0600-\u06FF]', ' ', s)  # keep arabic letters and digits underscore
    s = re.sub(r'[_]', ' ', s)
    s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip().lower()
    return s

full_span = "إِنَّمَا الْأَمْوَالُ تَزْدَادُ بِالْعَطَاءِ، وَتَخْفُضُ بِالْحُمْلِ"
norm_span = normalize_ar(full_span)
norm_span

def normalize_ar2(s):
    if s is None:
        return ''
    s = unicodedata.normalize('NFC', s)
    # unify letters
    s = s.replace('ٱ','ا').replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا')
    s = s.replace('ى','ي').replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي').replace('ة','ه')
    # remove diacritics and tatweel
    s = s.replace('ـ','')
    s = DIACRITICS_RE.sub('', s)
    # remove punctuation categories
    s = ''.join(ch if unicodedata.category(ch)[0] in ('L','N','Z') else ' ' for ch in s)
    s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip().lower()
    return s

norm_cache = []
for i,h in enumerate(hadith_data):
    text = h.get('Matn') or ''
    text2 = h.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    n1 = normalize_ar2(text)
    n2 = normalize_ar2(text2)
    norm_cache.append((i, n1, n2))

def find_contains(term):
    termn = normalize_ar2(term)
    res = []
    for i, n1, n2 in norm_cache:
        if termn in n1 or termn in n2:
            res.append(i)
    return res

for t in terms:
    ids = find_contains(t)
    print(t, len(ids), ids[:5])
